meaning: n.沟渠,堑壕,管沟,电缆沟,战壕v.掘沟,挖战壕
examples: "With virtual reality we'll be able to put a surgeon in every trench," said Satava.
This plate is growing on both sides, and since there is no intermediate trench, the two ridges must be moving apart.
The bridge lies like a trench in the fortress of the moat, which is shaped to blend in with the outlines of the landscape.
The trench itself is just visible from the gates, covered with a windowed white tent.
They had heard his plane and were safely in a slit trench when the bombs fell.
